articles.title: Chemotherapy Side Effects: What Adult Patients Can Expect
articles.summary: An overview of common chemotherapy side effects in adults, why they happen, and when to call your care team.
articles.body: Chemotherapy affects rapidly dividing cells throughout the body. This article explains common side effects such as fatigue, nausea, hair loss, and low blood counts, and offers practical tips for monitoring symptoms and staying in touch with your oncology team...

articles.title: Managing Chemotherapy Side Effects at Home: A Guide for Adults
articles.summary: Step-by-step strategies for adult patients to manage chemo side effects between clinic visits.
articles.body: Many chemotherapy side effects can be safely managed at home with the right tools and support. In this guide, we walk through setting up a symptom log, building a nausea management toolkit, staying hydrated, recognizing red-flag symptoms, and partnering with caregivers...

articles.title: Coping With Nausea, Vomiting, and Appetite Loss During Chemotherapy
articles.summary: Practical tips for adult patients dealing with gastrointestinal side effects of chemotherapy.
articles.body: Gastrointestinal side effects like nausea, vomiting, and appetite loss can make it harder to complete treatment. This article reviews medication options, eating strategies, hydration tips, and when to seek urgent care...

articles.title: Understanding the Long-Term Side Effects of Chemotherapy in Adults
articles.summary: Information on possible late and long-term effects of chemotherapy for adult survivors.
articles.body: While many chemotherapy side effects resolve after treatment ends, some can last months or years. This overview covers neuropathy, fertility changes, heart and lung effects, cognitive changes, and strategies for follow-up care...

articles.title: Beating Cancer-Related Fatigue During Chemotherapy
articles.summary: How adult patients can understand and manage fatigue throughout chemotherapy.
articles.body: Fatigue is one of the most common and distressing side effects of chemotherapy. Learn why fatigue occurs, how to pace your energy, when movement can help, and how to talk with your care team about extreme tiredness...

articles.title: Supporting an Adult Loved One Through Chemotherapy Side Effects
articles.summary: Guidance for caregivers on helping adult patients manage chemo side effects safely.
articles.body: Caregivers play a crucial role in monitoring side effects, tracking medications, and providing emotional support. This article explains what caregivers should watch for and how to partner effectively with the oncology team...

articles.title: Chemotherapy Side Effects in Children: What Parents Need to Know
articles.summary: A parent-focused overview of common chemotherapy side effects in children.
articles.body: Children experience many of the same chemotherapy side effects as adults, but they show up differently at different ages. This guide helps parents understand what is expected and what is an emergency...

articles.title: Nutrition During Chemotherapy: Eating Well When Nothing Sounds Good
articles.summary: Dietitian-approved strategies to support your body through chemotherapy.
articles.body: Chemotherapy can change how food tastes and how hungry you feel. This article offers practical meal ideas, snack suggestions, and hydration tips for adults in active treatment...

articles.title: Hair, Skin, and Nail Changes During Chemotherapy
articles.summary: What to expect and how to care for your body as treatment affects your appearance.
articles.body: This article explores hair loss, nail changes, and skin sensitivity that can occur with chemotherapy, plus tips for gentle care and talking about appearance changes with loved ones...

articles.title: Nerve Pain and Numbness From Chemotherapy: Understanding Neuropathy
articles.summary: Information for adults experiencing peripheral neuropathy during or after chemo.
articles.body: Peripheral neuropathy can cause tingling, burning, or numbness in the hands and feet. Learn why it happens, how it is treated, and what you can do at home to stay safe and comfortable...

articles.title: Chemotherapy Side Effects in Early-Stage Breast Cancer
articles.summary: What adults with early-stage breast cancer should know about chemo side effects.
articles.body: If you’re receiving chemotherapy for early-stage breast cancer, you may be wondering what side effects to expect and how long they’ll last. This article focuses on regimens commonly used in breast cancer and how to prepare...

articles.title: Chemotherapy Side Effects: Your Top 20 Questions Answered
articles.summary: Oncologists answer common questions from adults about chemo side effects.
articles.body: In this Q&A, oncology clinicians respond to frequently asked questions about timing, severity, and management of common chemotherapy side effects in adults...

articles.title: Chemotherapy Side Effects in Young Adults: School, Work, and Fertility
articles.summary: How chemo side effects uniquely impact young adults balancing school, work, and family planning.
articles.body: Young adults face unique challenges during chemotherapy, including navigating education, early careers, and fertility decisions. This article explains side effects in this life stage and offers planning tips...

articles.title: Radiation Therapy Side Effects: A Practical Overview
articles.summary: A general overview of short- and long-term side effects from radiation therapy.
articles.body: Radiation therapy is a local treatment but can still cause fatigue and skin changes. This article covers what to expect and how to care for treated skin...

articles.title: Immune-Related Side Effects of Immunotherapy
articles.summary: Recognizing and responding to immune-related side effects in adults on immunotherapy.
articles.body: Immunotherapy activates the immune system to attack cancer cells, but it can also affect healthy tissues. Learn the most common side effects, how they are graded, and why early reporting is essential...

articles.title: Video: Understanding Chemotherapy Side Effects
articles.summary: A 12-minute video for adults explaining how chemotherapy works and common side effects.

articles.title: Podcast: Caring for Someone Experiencing Chemo Side Effects
articles.summary: A conversation with caregivers about supporting loved ones during chemotherapy.

donationfunds.name: Pediatric Cancer Care Fund
donationfunds.description: Supports specialized medical care, child life services, and family support for children receiving cancer treatment.

donationfunds.name: General Cancer Care Fund
donationfunds.description: Provides flexible funding for the area of greatest need across all cancer programs.

donationfunds.name: Breast Cancer Research & Innovation Fund
donationfunds.description: Advances clinical trials and innovative therapies for patients with breast cancer.

donationfunds.name: Family Support Services Fund
donationfunds.description: Helps provide counseling, support groups, and respite care for families affected by cancer.

donationfunds.name: Transportation Assistance Fund
donationfunds.description: Offers gas cards, rideshare vouchers, and transit passes for patients traveling to treatment.

financialprograms.name: Caregiver Transportation & Medication Relief Fund
financialprograms.short_description: Financial assistance for caregivers with household income under $50,000 to cover treatment-related transportation and medication costs.
financialprograms.full_description: This program provides grants to caregivers of patients in active cancer treatment who have a documented household income under $50,000. Funds can be used for transportation to treatment appointments (gas, public transit, rideshare) and out-of-pocket prescription medication costs related to cancer care.
financialprograms.notes: Applicants may request support for both transportation and medication in a single application.

financialprograms.name: Patient Transportation Stipend Program
financialprograms.short_description: Travel stipends for patients with household income up to $75,000.
financialprograms.full_description: Provides monthly stipends to help cover travel costs for eligible patients attending chemotherapy, radiation, or follow-up appointments.
financialprograms.notes: Caregivers may be listed as secondary contacts but patients must apply.

financialprograms.name: Medication Co-pay Relief Fund
financialprograms.short_description: Helps patients with household incomes under $60,000 pay for cancer-related prescriptions.
financialprograms.full_description: Offers quarterly grants to reduce out-of-pocket prescription costs for cancer medications, including oral chemotherapy and supportive drugs.
financialprograms.notes: Prior authorization from the treating oncologist is required.

financialprograms.name: Pediatric Family Support and Travel Fund
financialprograms.short_description: Combined travel and lodging assistance for families of pediatric cancer patients.
financialprograms.full_description: Supports families traveling more than 25 miles for their child’s cancer treatment. Assistance may include gas cards, parking vouchers, and short-term lodging support.
financialprograms.notes: Available to legal guardians of pediatric patients receiving active treatment.

financialprograms.name: Rural Patient Travel Assistance Program
financialprograms.short_description: Travel reimbursement for rural patients with household income under $45,000.
financialprograms.full_description: Reimburses mileage and public transportation costs for rural patients traveling more than 50 miles each way to treatment.
financialprograms.notes: Program is currently paused due to funding limits.

financialprograms.name: Caregiver Respite Micro-Grants
financialprograms.short_description: Small grants to help caregivers cover respite care and related costs.
financialprograms.full_description: Provides one-time micro-grants that caregivers can use for respite care, childcare for siblings, or self-care activities while a loved one is in treatment.
financialprograms.notes: Does not cover transportation or medication costs.

financialprograms.name: Emergency Treatment Support Fund
financialprograms.short_description: Emergency support for essential expenses during intensive treatment.
financialprograms.full_description: Limited emergency funds to help with treatment-related costs such as transportation, medications, and temporary lodging during unplanned hospitalizations.
financialprograms.notes: Priority is given to those undergoing intensive inpatient treatment.

financialprograms.name: Bone Marrow Transplant Patient Assistance
financialprograms.short_description: Assistance for transplant patients during prolonged treatment.
financialprograms.full_description: Provides support for transportation, lodging, and select medication costs for patients undergoing bone marrow or stem cell transplant.
financialprograms.notes: Applicants must be within 90 days of transplant date.

financialprograms.name: Survivorship Wellness Grants
financialprograms.short_description: Wellness grants for survivors to engage in post-treatment health programs.
financialprograms.full_description: Offers small grants for gym memberships, counseling, and nutrition services for those who have completed active treatment.
financialprograms.notes: Not intended for emergency financial needs.

financialprograms.name: Pharmacy Bridge Program
financialprograms.short_description: Short-term help with cancer medication costs during insurance gaps.
financialprograms.full_description: Provides up to 90 days of assistance for cancer-related prescriptions when patients experience a temporary gap in coverage.
financialprograms.notes: Currently closed; expected to reopen later this year.

giftcategories.name: Comfort Items
giftcategories.description: Soft blankets, pillows, and other items that bring physical comfort to patients during treatment.

giftcategories.name: Nutrition & Snacks
giftcategories.description: Nutritious snacks and meal vouchers that help patients stay nourished through treatment.

giftcategories.name: Transportation Support
giftcategories.description: Bus passes, gas cards, and rideshare credits that help patients get to and from treatment safely.

giftcategories.name: Other Meaningful Gifts
giftcategories.description: Additional items and services that improve quality of life for patients and families.

services.name: Downtown Breast Cancer Counseling Center
services.description: In-person individual and family counseling for adults diagnosed with breast cancer, located steps from the main cancer center.
services.eligibility_notes: Open to adults with a current or past breast cancer diagnosis. Priority for patients receiving care within 25 miles of San Francisco.

services.name: East Bay Breast Cancer Counseling Clinic
services.description: Short-term counseling and support groups for adults with breast cancer and their families.
services.eligibility_notes: In-person services for residents within 30 miles of Oakland.

services.name: Walnut Creek Breast Cancer Support & Counseling
services.description: On-site counseling and small support groups for adults living with or beyond breast cancer.
services.eligibility_notes: Serves patients in Contra Costa County and surrounding areas.

services.name: California Virtual Breast Cancer Counseling
services.description: Online counseling sessions for adults with breast cancer anywhere in California.
services.eligibility_notes: Available to adults residing in California with a confirmed breast cancer diagnosis.

services.name: General Oncology Counseling - Main Campus
services.description: Comprehensive counseling for adults with any cancer diagnosis and their loved ones.
services.eligibility_notes: Open to patients and caregivers currently or previously treated at Hope Oncology Center.

services.name: Pediatric Leukemia Family Navigation Service
services.description: Navigation and psychosocial support for families of children with leukemia.
services.eligibility_notes: For families of pediatric leukemia patients receiving care at our children’s hospital.

services.name: Cancer Financial Counseling - Main Campus
services.description: Financial counselors help patients and caregivers understand bills, insurance, and assistance programs.
services.eligibility_notes: Appointments recommended; walk-ins accepted as availability allows.

services.name: San Francisco Community Mammography Clinic
services.description: Screening mammograms and follow-up diagnostic services.
services.eligibility_notes: Screening for women and some men age 40+ or younger with risk factors.

services.name: National Cancer Information Helpline
services.description: Telephone support for questions about diagnosis, treatment, and coping.
services.eligibility_notes: Free and confidential for anyone in the U.S.

services.name: In-Person Breast Cancer Support Group - City Campus
services.description: Weekly drop-in support group for adults in any stage of breast cancer treatment.
services.eligibility_notes: No registration required; check-in at the Wellness Center front desk.

services.name: Telehealth Psych-Oncology Service
services.description: Licensed clinicians provide video-based mental health care for adults with any cancer type.
services.eligibility_notes: Available to adults residing in California, Oregon, and Washington.

services.name: Childhood Cancer Family Support Center
services.description: Supportive services for families of children with any cancer type, including play therapy and parent groups.
services.eligibility_notes: Family members of pediatric oncology patients only.

services.name: Mobile Cancer Screening Van
services.description: Mobile screening services for underserved neighborhoods in the Bay Area.
services.hours: Hours vary by location; see website for schedule.
services.eligibility_notes: Priority for uninsured and underinsured adults.

services.name: Spanish-Language Patient Navigation
services.description: Navigation and education services in Spanish for adults with cancer and their families.
services.eligibility_notes: Services provided in Spanish; interpreters available for other languages.

stories.title: Finding My Strength: Maya’s Lymphoma Journey at 24
stories.body: At 24, Maya was balancing graduate school and a new job when a persistent cough led to a lymphoma diagnosis. In this story, she shares how she navigated treatment, side effects, and the emotional rollercoaster of young adulthood with cancer...

stories.title: Back to the Climbing Wall: Jordan’s Hodgkin Lymphoma Story
stories.body: Jordan was 29 and training for a climbing trip when swollen lymph nodes changed everything. He describes facing treatment, asking for help, and slowly rebuilding strength after remission...

stories.title: Redefining Strong: Ana’s Breast Cancer Story
stories.body: Ana talks about parenting two teenagers while moving through surgery, chemotherapy, and radiation for breast cancer...

stories.title: Superhero Capes and Chemo Days: Daniel’s Leukemia Story
stories.body: Daniel’s family shares how they used play and imagination to get through long days in the hospital...

stories.title: Dating, Disclosures, and Lymphoma: Rachel’s Story
stories.body: Rachel, diagnosed with lymphoma at 34, opens up about body image, dating during treatment, and finding community online...

stories.title: A New Chapter at 67: Grace’s Survivorship Story
stories.body: After completing treatment for colon cancer, Grace focused on redefining retirement and finding new ways to give back...

stories.title: From Dorm Room to Infusion Chair: College With Lymphoma
stories.body: Alex describes pausing college mid-semester for treatment, staying connected with friends, and returning to campus after remission...

stories.title: My Mom’s Chemo, My Senior Year
stories.body: Lena shares what it was like to finish high school while her mom underwent chemotherapy for breast cancer...

stories.title: Back in the Classroom After Leukemia
stories.body: Marcus, a middle school teacher, describes his acute leukemia diagnosis, transplant journey, and return to teaching...

stories.title: Learning to Ask for Help: A Parent’s Perspective on Childhood Cancer
stories.body: Sofia reflects on caregiving for her 6-year-old with leukemia, balancing work and hospital stays, and finding community in a support group...

stories.title: Working Remote Through Lymphoma Treatment
stories.body: Priya shares how switching to remote work made it possible to continue her career while going through chemotherapy for lymphoma...

stories.title: Why I Volunteer in the Chemo Lounge
stories.body: Helen, a breast cancer survivor, now volunteers in the chemotherapy infusion area to support others starting treatment...

supportgroups.name: National Pediatric Leukemia Caregivers Circle
supportgroups.description: A weekly virtual support group for caregivers of children with leukemia, offering space to share challenges and coping strategies.

supportgroups.name: West Coast Caregivers of Children With Leukemia
supportgroups.description: Weekly online group for parents and primary caregivers of children receiving treatment for leukemia at West Coast centers.

supportgroups.name: San Francisco In-Person Breast Cancer Support Group
supportgroups.description: Weekly in-person group for adults with any stage of breast cancer.
supportgroups.typical_meeting_time: Wednesdays 6:00–7:30 PM PT at Main Campus

supportgroups.name: Young Adults Living With and Beyond Lymphoma
supportgroups.description: Virtual group for young adults ages 18–39 who are in treatment for lymphoma or in remission.
supportgroups.typical_meeting_time: Every other Monday 5:00–6:15 PM PT (via Zoom)

supportgroups.name: Caregivers of Adults With Cancer Online Support Group
supportgroups.description: A mixed-diagnosis group for adult caregivers supporting a loved one with any cancer type.

supportgroups.name: Men Facing Cancer Together
supportgroups.description: Monthly in-person group for adult men with any cancer diagnosis.
supportgroups.typical_meeting_time: First Thursday of each month, 6:00–7:30 PM PT

supportgroups.name: Grupo en Línea para Cuidadores (Español)
supportgroups.description: Online support group in Spanish for caregivers of adults with cancer.

supportgroups.name: Teen Siblings of Kids With Cancer
supportgroups.description: Support group for teenagers who have a brother or sister in cancer treatment.
supportgroups.typical_meeting_time: Every other Thursday 4:30–5:30 PM PT (via Zoom)

supportgroups.name: Newly Diagnosed Breast Cancer Orientation Session
supportgroups.description: One-time educational and support session for those newly diagnosed with breast cancer.
supportgroups.typical_meeting_time: First Monday of every month, 2:00–3:30 PM PT (via Zoom)

volunteeropportunities.title: Patient Support Ambassador – Main Campus
volunteeropportunities.description: Greet patients, offer warm blankets and beverages, and provide wayfinding support in the main hospital lobby and infusion areas.
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Must be 18 or older and complete volunteer orientation and health screening.

volunteeropportunities.title: Weekend Patient Transport Volunteer – Main Campus
volunteeropportunities.description: Assist staff by safely escorting patients in wheelchairs to appointments within the main hospital.
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Comfortable walking and pushing wheelchairs; training provided.

volunteeropportunities.title: Family Check-In Desk Volunteer – Main Campus
volunteeropportunities.description: Welcome families, answer basic questions, and help them sign in for visits.
volunteeropportunities.location_name: Hope Oncology Center – Family Resource Center
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Ideal for friendly, organized volunteers comfortable working with families under stress.

volunteeropportunities.title: Infusion Companion – Main Campus
volunteeropportunities.description: Sit with patients during chemotherapy infusions, offer conversation, reading materials, or quiet presence as requested.
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Interview and additional training required; must commit to a regular shift.

volunteeropportunities.title: 5K Run/Walk Event Support Volunteer
volunteeropportunities.description: Help with setup, registration, and water stations at our annual 5K fundraising event.
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: One-day commitment; great for groups and corporate teams.

volunteeropportunities.title: Weekday Administrative Volunteer – Main Campus
volunteeropportunities.description: Assist with data entry, filing, and mailings in the volunteer services office.
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Basic computer skills required; suitable for low-activity roles.

volunteeropportunities.title: Remote Information Helpline Volunteer
volunteeropportunities.description: Answer non-medical questions via phone and email from home, providing information about our programs and services.
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Requires stable internet connection and completion of remote volunteer training.

volunteeropportunities.title: Patient Support Volunteer – East Bay Clinic
volunteeropportunities.description: Provide hospitality and non-clinical support to patients at our satellite infusion clinic.
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Orientation required at main campus plus on-site training at clinic.

volunteeropportunities.title: Pediatric Playroom Volunteer
volunteeropportunities.description: Engage children in age-appropriate play and creative activities in the pediatric oncology playroom.
volunteeropportunities.location_name: Hope Children’s Cancer Center – Main Campus
volunteeropportunities.eligibility_notes: Additional health and background screenings required; minimum 6-month commitment.

giftitems.name: Cozy Chemotherapy Blanket
giftitems.description: A soft, oversized fleece blanket that helps keep patients warm during long infusion appointments.
giftitems.impact_statement: Your gift provides warmth and comfort to a patient during treatment days.

giftitems.name: Soothing Neck Pillow
giftitems.description: A supportive neck pillow designed to ease tension and improve comfort in waiting rooms and infusion chairs.
giftitems.impact_statement: Helps relieve physical strain and makes treatment days a little easier.

giftitems.name: Warm Gripper Socks (3-Pack)
giftitems.description: Non-slip, warm socks that help patients feel secure and cozy while walking in the hospital.
giftitems.impact_statement: Prevents falls and keeps patients comfortable on chilly hospital floors.

giftitems.name: Deluxe Comfort Kit
giftitems.description: A bundle including a blanket, eye mask, lip balm, and hand lotion for long treatment days.
giftitems.impact_statement: Provides a full comfort package to patients starting intensive chemotherapy.

giftitems.name: Protein Snack Box
giftitems.description: A curated box of high-protein, easy-to-open snacks chosen for people in treatment.
giftitems.impact_statement: Ensures patients have nourishing options even on days when cooking feels impossible.

giftitems.name: Gentle Herbal Tea Assortment
giftitems.description: A selection of caffeine-free teas that can soothe nausea and dry mouth.
giftitems.impact_statement: Offers warmth and gentle relief for patients coping with chemotherapy side effects.

giftitems.name: Fresh Fruit Market Voucher
giftitems.description: A voucher that patients can redeem at partner markets for fresh fruits and vegetables.
giftitems.impact_statement: Helps patients access fresh, appealing foods during treatment.

giftitems.name: One-Week Nutritional Shake Supply
giftitems.description: A week of balanced nutritional shakes for patients struggling with appetite or weight loss.
giftitems.impact_statement: Supports patients in maintaining strength when solid foods are hard to tolerate.

giftitems.name: Local Bus Pass to Treatment
giftitems.description: A preloaded public transit card covering several round trips to the cancer center.
giftitems.impact_statement: Removes a critical barrier so patients can reliably reach lifesaving care.

giftitems.name: Gas Card for Treatment Visits
giftitems.description: A gas card that helps offset the cost of driving to frequent appointments.
giftitems.impact_statement: Helps families who travel long distances keep fuel in the tank for every visit.

giftitems.name: Rideshare Credit to Treatment
giftitems.description: Prepaid rideshare credits for patients who cannot drive or safely use public transit.
giftitems.impact_statement: Provides safe, door-to-door transportation for vulnerable patients.

giftitems.name: Monthly Parking Pass for Caregivers
giftitems.description: A parking pass for the hospital garage to ease the financial strain of frequent visits.
giftitems.impact_statement: Lifts a hidden financial burden for families at the hospital day after day.

giftitems.name: Art Therapy Kit
giftitems.description: A set of sketchbooks, colored pencils, and watercolors for creative expression during treatment.
giftitems.impact_statement: Gives patients a calming outlet to process emotions and pass the time.

giftitems.name: Guided Journaling Set
giftitems.description: A guided journal with prompts, plus pens, to help patients reflect and track their journey.
giftitems.impact_statement: Supports emotional well-being by encouraging reflection and hope.

giftitems.name: Family Night Activity Pack
giftitems.description: Board games, card decks, and conversation starters for families spending time together during or after treatment.
giftitems.impact_statement: Creates moments of connection and laughter on difficult days.

giftitems.name: Interpreter Services Fund Contribution
giftitems.description: Supports professional medical interpreters for patients and families who speak languages other than English.
giftitems.impact_statement: Ensures every patient can understand their care plan in their preferred language.

volunteershifts.notes: Main lobby and infusion area; weekend morning welcoming shift.
Ideal for first-time volunteers; includes brief on-shift orientation.
Morning lobby ambassador shift with focus on new patient check-ins.
Assist with early-morning diagnostic and infusion appointments.
Wheelchair transport within main campus buildings.
Morning transport support; moderate walking required.
Family Resource Center front desk; training provided at shift start.
Support higher weekend visitor volume at the Family Resource Center.
Play-based support for pediatric patients; child life specialist on-site.
Morning creative arts and games in the pediatric playroom.
Remote shift; volunteers answer general questions and route callers.
Afternoon office support in Volunteer Services.
Morning filing and data entry; quiet office environment.
Day-of 5K Run/Walk support at Crissy Field; outdoor role, rain or shine.
East Bay satellite clinic hospitality and wayfinding support.
Weekend morning infusion companion shift in the chemotherapy suite.

eventregistrations.notes: Requests materials via email after session.
Bringing a friend; requests accessible seating.

events.title: Nutrition During Chemotherapy: Evening Q&A With the Dietitian
events.description: A live, interactive webinar for adult patients and caregivers on what to eat before, during, and after chemotherapy sessions, with time for questions.

events.title: Eating Well Through Chemo: Evening Meal Planning Workshop
events.description: A free online workshop focused on practical meal and snack ideas for people currently receiving chemotherapy.

events.title: Lunchtime Briefing: Managing Nausea With Food Choices
events.description: A 45-minute lunchtime webinar on how specific food choices can ease nausea during chemotherapy.

events.title: Staying Nourished During Chemo: Advanced Tips
events.description: An in-depth webinar for patients who have already completed a basic nutrition during chemotherapy class and want more tailored strategies.

events.title: Online Workshop: Financial Assistance for Caregivers
events.description: A practical overview of transportation, medication, and respite assistance programs available to caregivers of people in cancer treatment.

events.title: Hope Survivorship Forum: Life After Cancer Treatment
events.description: An in-person half-day forum at the main campus with panels on work, exercise, mental health, and long-term side effects after treatment.
events.location_name: Hope Oncology Center  Main Campus Auditorium

events.title: Pre-Op Education Class for Breast Surgery Patients
events.description: A nurse-led, in-person group class for patients scheduled for breast surgery, covering what to expect before and after the operation.

events.title: Orientation: Finding the Right Support Group for You
events.description: An online orientation session that explains available support groups (patients, caregivers, and families) and how to join.

events.title: Volunteer Orientation: Main Campus and Remote Roles
events.description: Required orientation session for new volunteers interested in patient support, family check-in, transport, and remote helpline roles.
events.location_name: Hope Oncology Center  Volunteer Services + Online

events.title: Family Day at the Cancer Center
events.description: A relaxed weekend event with kid-friendly activities, tours, and talks for families affected by cancer.
events.location_name: Hope Oncology Center  Courtyard and Lobby
